unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Computer
title: Writing a Document Online
drDigitalIntro:
Google Docs is a free word processor that runs in your web browser — no software to install.

Try it now on your own computer:

1. Open your web browser.
2. Go to docs.google.com (you'll need to sign in with a Google account — if you don't have one, you can create one for free).
3. Click the + Blank button to create a new document.
4. Type a short paragraph about anything you like — your favorite meal, a memory, what you did today.
5. Watch the top of the page: it says "Saving..." then "All changes saved." Your work is saved automatically — no need to click Save!

That's it — you just created a document that lives in the cloud and can be opened from any device.
drDigitalSuccess: You created an online document that saves itself! Google Docs works just like a regular word processor, but your file lives on the internet so you can open it from any computer.
drDigitalHint: Open your browser, go to docs.google.com, click Blank, and start typing.
